articles.summary: NASA's daring Swift Boost mission launches June 27 to save the nearly 22-year-old Swift observatory , which is being dragged out of space by Earth's atmosphere.

articles.title: Primate evolution kept aging rates stable for 25 million years despite lifespan gaps
articles.summary: Biologists group animals with similar traits into broad categories called orders. Despite their similarities, animal species in the same order can have very different average lifespans.

articles.title: Scientists uncover evolutionary edge behind plant invasions
articles.summary: Plants that become invasive may owe their success to an advantage shaped long before they arrive, according to new research led by King's College London.

articles.title: UK sets new June temperature record for third day in a row: Met Office
articles.summary: The UK broke the record for a June temperature for the third day in a row on Friday, the Met Office weather agency said, as a sweltering heat wave strained schools and hospitals and drove down business.

articles.title: Germany sees hottest temperature on record of 41.3C: Weather service
articles.summary: Germany saw its highest temperature ever recorded Friday at 41.3°C (106.3°F), according to preliminary weather service data, as the country braces for the possibility of even higher temperatures over the weekend.

articles.summary: The loss of Antarctica’s doomsday glacier would transform our planet. Now scientists are revealing the secrets of this remotest of places, and asking the question: is its demise inevitable?

articles.title: Climate change to blame for intensity of Europe heat wave: Scientists

articles.title: Swiss glaciers facing drastic loss from heat wave: Expert
articles.summary: Swiss glaciers are set to lose an enormous amount of ice due to the heat wave battering Europe, the head of Glacier Monitoring in Switzerland (GLAMOS) told AFP.

articles.title: Farmers fear drought as Italy's longest river runs dry
articles.summary: Seawater is seeping into Italy's longest river as the waterway starts to run dry in the heat wave, hitting a farming heartland that produces the milk for Parmesan cheese.

articles.title: Europe’s heatwave is the hottest and most humid ever
articles.summary: The current temperatures in western and central Europe would have been virtually impossible 50 years ago, and unprecedented humidity levels make this heatwave especially dangerous
